name: Associated Credit Union
description_short: Associated Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering personal and business banking, lending, and investment products through online and branch locations.
description_long:
Associated Credit Union (ACU) operates as a traditional credit union, providing comprehensive financial services to both personal and business members. The organization is structured as a member-owned cooperative, distinguishing it from for-profit banks, and maintains a physical branch network alongside digital banking capabilities.

ACU's service portfolio spans multiple financial categories. On the banking side, they offer personal and business checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with both regular and IRA options. Their lending division includes auto loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, personal loans, student loans, recreational vehicle loans, business loans, and credit card products. Members can access online banking platforms, mobile apps (ACU Wallet App and ACU Mobile App), and loan payment services including a loan payment deferral program.

What distinguishes ACU is their emphasis on member benefits and community engagement. The credit union operates the ACU Foundation, offers member rewards programs, hosts financial education resources including calculators and planning tools, and provides business development support. They advertise competitive rates across product categories and superior service as core value propositions. The organization maintains a routing number (261171338) and actively recruits new members through membership applications and appointment booking.

Based on the available website content, ACU appears to be a well-established regional or national credit union with comprehensive offerings. However, the website content provided is primarily navigation and login prompts rather than detailed product information, rates, or membership requirements. Prospective members should visit the full website or contact ACU directly to understand specific eligibility criteria, current rates, fees, and complete service details before joining.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers competitive rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Comprehensive service range including personal banking, business banking, lending, and investment products
Multiple digital channels: online banking platform, ACU Wallet App, and ACU Mobile App for account access
Community-focused initiatives including ACU Foundation, member rewards programs, and financial education resources
Business banking services with dedicated business checking, savings, and lending products
Loan flexibility options including payment deferral programs for members in financial difficulty
Physical branch locations combined with online services for omnichannel banking access
cons: Website content does not clearly specify membership eligibility requirements or geographic service area
Limited rate and fee information available on the locations page; detailed pricing requires further navigation
No information provided about deposit insurance coverage (NCUA) or member protections
Website structure shows repeated login/application prompts but lacks transparent fee schedules or product comparisons
No mention of account opening requirements, minimum balances, or specific APR ranges for products
best_for: Individuals seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive rates
Small business owners needing comprehensive business banking and lending solutions
Members prioritizing financial education, community involvement, and member rewards programs
Borrowers interested in auto loans, mortgages, or personal loans from a credit union
diagnosis: Associated Credit Union is best for individuals and businesses seeking comprehensive banking and lending services from a member-owned financial institution. The primary caveat is that specific membership eligibility, service areas, current rates, and fees are not clearly detailed on the locations page provided; potential members must explore the full website or contact ACU directly for complete information before applying.
services: Certificates of deposit (CDs) with regular and IRA options
Mobile banking apps (ACU Wallet and ACU Mobile)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2250 N Druid Hills Rd, Atlanta, GA 30329
